---
title: "TV animal deaths"
---

## Query the Does the Dog Die database

This script queries the Does the Dog Die? database for movies in the IMDB top 1000 list that are (probably) American and released on or after 2000.

It then repeats this analysis for the top 2000 TV series.

```{r}

# Load libraries

library(httr)
library(jsonlite)
library(tidyverse)
library(janitor)

```

## Helper functions

```{r}

# Function to query the Does The Dog Die database
query_dtdd_database <- function(movie_title) {
  resp <- GET(
    url = "https://www.doesthedogdie.com/dddsearch",
    query = list(q = movie_title),
    add_headers(`Accept` = "application/json", `X-API-KEY` = Sys.getenv("DDD_API_KEY"))
  )
  
  if (status_code(resp) == 200) {
    data <- fromJSON(content(resp, "text", encoding = "UTF-8"), flatten = TRUE)
    if (length(data$items) > 0) {
      return(as_tibble(data$items))
    } else {
      return(tibble(title = movie_title, message = "No results found"))
    }
  } else {
    return(tibble(title = movie_title, message = "Error querying API"))
  }
}

# Function to query Does the Dog Die database by IMDB ID
query_dtdd_by_imdb_id <- function(imdb_id) {
  resp <- GET(
    url = "https://www.doesthedogdie.com/dddsearch",
    query = list(imdb = imdb_id),
    add_headers(`Accept` = "application/json", `X-API-KEY` = Sys.getenv("DDD_API_KEY"))
  )
  
  if (status_code(resp) == 200) {
    data <- fromJSON(content(resp, "text", encoding = "UTF-8"), flatten = TRUE)
    if (length(data$items) > 0) {
      return(as_tibble(data$items))
    } else {
      return(tibble(imdb_id = imdb_id, message = "No results found"))
    }
  } else {
    return(tibble(imdb_id = imdb_id, message = "Error querying API"))
  }
}

# Function to fetch details for a specific movie
fetch_dtdd_movie_details <- function(item_id) {
  resp <- GET(
    url = paste0("https://www.doesthedogdie.com/media/", item_id),
    add_headers(`Accept` = "application/json", `X-API-KEY` = Sys.getenv("DDD_API_KEY"))
  )
  
  if (status_code(resp) == 200) {
    data <- fromJSON(content(resp, "text", encoding = "UTF-8"), flatten = TRUE)
    # extract the relevant data
    # 1. a dog dies yes votes and no votes
    # To do this, I first identify the topic ID associated with "a dog dies"
    dogVotes <- data$topicItemStats |> filter(tolower(topic.name) == "a dog dies") |> 
      select(topic.name, yesSum, noSum)
    # rename the yes and no columns to denote that they apply only to dogs
    dogVotes <- dogVotes |> 
      rename(dog_yes_votes = yesSum, dog_no_votes = noSum)
    
    # 2. a cat dies yes votes and no votes
    catVotes <- data$topicItemStats |> filter(tolower(topic.name) == "a cat dies") |> 
      select(topic.name, yesSum, noSum)
    # rename the yes and no columns to denote that they apply only to cats
    catVotes <- catVotes |> 
      rename(cat_yes_votes = yesSum, cat_no_votes = noSum)
    
    # 3. an animal dies yes votes and no votes
    animalVotes <- data$topicItemStats |> filter(tolower(topic.name) == "an animal dies") |> 
      select(topic.name, yesSum, noSum)
    # rename the yes and no columns to denote that they apply to all animals
    animalVotes <- animalVotes |> 
      rename(animal_yes_votes = yesSum, animal_no_votes = noSum)
    
    # Join the votes data together with a column for the name of the movie
    data <- tibble(
      #id = data$item$id,
      #name = data$item$name,
      #clean_name = data$item$cleanName,
      dog_yes_votes = dogVotes$dog_yes_votes,
      dog_no_votes = dogVotes$dog_no_votes,
      cat_yes_votes = catVotes$cat_yes_votes,
      cat_no_votes = catVotes$cat_no_votes,
      animal_yes_votes = animalVotes$animal_yes_votes,
      animal_no_votes = animalVotes$animal_no_votes
    )
    
    return(data)
  } else {
    return(tibble(message = "Error fetching details"))
  }
}
```

## Top 5000 movies

```{r}
# # Read in IMDB 1000 data set
# # Filter out movies that didn't air in the last 20 years (only movies released on or after 2000)
# imdb_1000_data <- read_csv("data/imdb_top_1000.csv") |> 
#   # Filter out movies for which Released Year isn't a valid 4 digit year
#   filter(Released_Year %in% as.character(1900:2025)) |>
#   #mutate(Released_Year = parse_number(Released_Year)) |> 
#   mutate(Released_Year = as.numeric(Released_Year)) |>
#   filter(Released_Year >= 2000)

# Read in the IMDB 1000 recent datasets
# imdb_1000_us_recent <- read_csv("data/imdb_1000_us_recent.csv")
# imdb_1000_recent <- read_csv("data/imdb_1000_recent.csv")

# Read in the IMDB 5000 movies data set
# Source: https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/tiagoadrianunes/imdb-top-5000-movies
imdb_movies_5000 <- read_csv("data/imdb_movies_5000.csv")

```



### Query the database and pull the data

```{r}
# For each movie in the set of recent movies, query the Does The Dog Die database
# and pull out info from the movie. 
# 
# If the movie doesn't exist in the database (or there are no results), skip it.
# Put the data from all movies into one tibble.

results_all_movies <- imdb_movies_5000 |>
  #slice_head(n = 50) |> # NOTE: This line is for debugging purposes only
  mutate(dtdd_data = map(tconst, query_dtdd_by_imdb_id)) |>
  unnest(dtdd_data) |> 
  filter(!is.na(id)) |> # Filter out movies that don't have an ID in the database
  filter(startYear == releaseYear) # Filter out movies that don't have a matching year
                                      # This eliminates movies that have been remade
                                      # and movies that have matching names


```

```{r}
# Save the results
write_csv(results_all_movies, "data/imdb_5000_dtdd_results.csv")
```

```{r}
# Fetch the movie details for each movie in the results using the id
# and save all the results into a new tibble

results_movie_details <- results_all_movies |>
  #slice_head(n = 10) |> # NOTE: This line is for debugging purposes only
  mutate(dtdd_details = map(id, fetch_dtdd_movie_details)) |>
  unnest(dtdd_details) |>
  select(id, name, cleanName, 
         dog_yes_votes, dog_no_votes,
         cat_yes_votes, cat_no_votes,
         animal_yes_votes, animal_no_votes)
```

```{r}
# Save the movie details
write_csv(results_movie_details, "data/imdb_5000_dtdd_movie_details.csv")
```

### Analyze the data


```{r}
# Create new columns in results_movie_details that calculate 
# the majority vote for each animal type and set to 1 if
# the majority vote is "yes" and 0 if the majority vote is "no".
# Set the value to 0 if there is no majority vote (i.e. if the yes and no votes are equal).
results_movie_details <- results_movie_details |>
  mutate(dog_death = case_when(
    dog_yes_votes > dog_no_votes ~ TRUE,
    TRUE ~ FALSE),
    cat_death = case_when(
      cat_yes_votes > cat_no_votes ~ TRUE,
      TRUE ~ FALSE),
    animal_death = case_when(
      animal_yes_votes > animal_no_votes ~ TRUE,
      TRUE ~ FALSE)
  )
```

```{r}
# Compute the percentage of movies that have:
# 1. dog deaths
dog_death_percentage <- results_movie_details |> 
  summarise(dog_death_pct = mean(dog_death, na.rm = TRUE) * 100)
print(dog_death_percentage)
# 2. cat deaths
cat_death_percentage <- results_movie_details |> 
  summarise(cat_death_pct = mean(cat_death, na.rm = TRUE) * 100)
print(cat_death_percentage)
# 3. animal deaths
animal_death_percentage <- results_movie_details |> 
  summarise(animal_death_pct = mean(animal_death, na.rm = TRUE) * 100)
print(animal_death_percentage)
```
```{r}
# Compute the correlation between a movie having a dog death and 
# having an animal death
correlation_dog_animal <- cor(results_movie_details$dog_death, 
                              results_movie_details$animal_death, use = "complete.obs")
print(correlation_dog_animal)

# Repeat for the correlation between a movie having a cat death and
# having an animal death
correlation_cat_animal <- cor(results_movie_details$cat_death, 
                              results_movie_details$animal_death, use = "complete.obs")
print(correlation_cat_animal)
```

## Top 5000 TV series

```{r}
# Read in the IMDB 2000 TV series data set
imdb_tvseries_2000 <- read_csv("data/imdb_tvseries_2000.csv")

# Read in the IMDB 5000 TV series data set
# Source: https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/tiagoadrianunes/imdb-top-5000-tv-shows
imdb_tvseries_5000 <- read_csv("data/imdb_tvseries_5000.csv")
```

### Query the database

```{r}
# For each series in the set of 5000 top TV series, query the Does The Dog Die database
# and pull out info from the series. 
# 
# If the series doesn't exist in the database (or there are no results), skip it.
# Put the data from all series into one tibble.

results_all_tvseries <- imdb_tvseries_5000 |>
  #slice_head(n = 50) |> # NOTE: This line is for debugging purposes only
  mutate(dtdd_data = map(primaryTitle, query_dtdd_database)) |>
  unnest(dtdd_data) |>
  # Filter out shows that don't have at least 100 ratings (arbitrary cutoff)
  # The idea here is that shows with few ratings either have unreliable stats
  # or are obsure or not valid matches
  filter(numRatings > 100) |> 
  # filter out shows that don't match years to eliminate partial matches
  # or books and movies with the same or similar titles
  filter(startYear == releaseYear) |> 
  # As an additional check, I will filter out entries that aren't classified
  # as TV shows
  filter(itemType.name == "TV Show")
           
```

```{r}
# Save the results
write_csv(results_all_tvseries, "data/imdb_tvseries_5000_dtdd_results.csv")
```

```{r}
# Fetch the series details for each series in the results using the id
# and save all the results into a new tibble

results_tvseries_details <- results_all_tvseries |>
  #slice_head(n = 10) |> # NOTE: This line is for debugging purposes only
  mutate(dtdd_details = map(id, fetch_dtdd_movie_details)) |>
  unnest(dtdd_details) |>
  select(id, name, cleanName, 
         dog_yes_votes, dog_no_votes,
         cat_yes_votes, cat_no_votes,
         animal_yes_votes, animal_no_votes)
```


```{r}
# Save the series details
write_csv(results_tvseries_details, "data/imdb_tvseries_5000_dtdd_series_details.csv")
```

### Analyze the data

```{r}
# Create new columns in results_tvseries_details that calculate 
# the majority vote for each animal type and set to 1 if
# the majority vote is "yes" and 0 if the majority vote is "no".
# Set the value to 0 if there is no majority vote (i.e. if the yes and no votes are equal).
results_tvseries_details <- results_tvseries_details |>
  mutate(dog_death = case_when(
    dog_yes_votes > dog_no_votes ~ TRUE,
    TRUE ~ FALSE),
    cat_death = case_when(
      cat_yes_votes > cat_no_votes ~ TRUE,
      TRUE ~ FALSE),
    animal_death = case_when(
      animal_yes_votes > animal_no_votes ~ TRUE,
      TRUE ~ FALSE)
  )
```

```{r}
# Compute the percentage of TV series that have:
# 1. dog deaths
dog_death_percentage <- results_tvseries_details |> 
  summarise(dog_death_pct = mean(dog_death, na.rm = TRUE) * 100)
print(dog_death_percentage)
# 2. cat deaths
cat_death_percentage <- results_tvseries_details |> 
  summarise(cat_death_pct = mean(cat_death, na.rm = TRUE) * 100)
print(cat_death_percentage)
# 3. animal deaths
animal_death_percentage <- results_tvseries_details |> 
  summarise(animal_death_pct = mean(animal_death, na.rm = TRUE) * 100)
print(animal_death_percentage)
```
```{r}
# Compute the correlation between a TV series having a dog death and 
# having an animal death
correlation_dog_animal <- cor(results_tvseries_details$dog_death, 
                              results_tvseries_details$animal_death, use = "complete.obs")
print(correlation_dog_animal)

# Repeat for the correlation between a TV series having a cat death and
# having an animal death
correlation_cat_animal <- cor(results_tvseries_details$cat_death, 
                              results_tvseries_details$animal_death, use = "complete.obs")
print(correlation_cat_animal)
```
